data.title: XtalPi Flags Interim Loss as R&D Push Weighs on Earnings
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from QuantumPharm, Inc. ( (HK:2228) ).

XtalPi Holdings Limited has warned investors that it expects to swing to a net loss of about RMB215 million to RMB275 million for the six months ended June 30, 2026, compared with a net profit of roughly RMB82.8 million a year earlier. The company also anticipates a decline in headline revenue to around RMB380 million to RMB400 million, largely due to a high comparison base created by a major pipeline licensing upfront payment booked in the prior period.

Excluding that one-off licensing effect, XtalPi projects at least RMB250 million in revenue, more than 65% higher year-on-year, with its AI for Science intelligent solutions set to grow over 120% to no less than RMB180 million. The group attributes the loss to a more than 50% increase in research and development spending to at least RMB340 million, as it accelerates investment in autonomous labs, agentic systems and diversified drug technology platforms, a strategy it believes will reinforce long-term R&D strength and sustain future revenue growth.

More about QuantumPharm, Inc.

XtalPi Holdings Limited operates in the AI-driven drug discovery and scientific research sector, providing intelligent solutions, robotic laboratory systems and related services to pharmaceutical and biotech clients. The group focuses on Scientific AI, Physical AI and agentic systems, and is expanding into proprietary drug pipelines and multimodal technology platforms spanning small and large molecules, molecular glues, peptides and oligonucleotides.

The company is pursuing a strategic shift from pure service provision toward building asset-based platforms and pipelines with long-term commercialization potential. It has launched XtalPi Science, an open intelligent R&D platform aimed at scientific research scenarios, and is investing heavily in autonomous laboratories and integrated AI systems to create a closed-loop research and development ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 58,873,850

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$30.11B

See more data about 2228 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: GSK Discloses July 22 Executive ADS Acquisition by U.S. President
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

GlaxoSmithKline ( (GSK) ) has issued an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, GSK notified the market of a personal dealing transaction by Maya Martinez-Davis, its President, US, involving the acquisition of notional American Depositary Shares through her Executive Supplemental Savings Plan account. The transaction, executed on the New York Stock Exchange at a price of $50.76 per ADS for a volume of 9.407 ADS, reflects standard participation by a senior U.S. executive in the company’s long-term savings and incentive arrangements.

The disclosure, formally filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on July 24, 2026, underscores GSK’s adherence to regulatory requirements governing transactions by persons discharging managerial responsibilities. While the scale of the acquisition is modest, such filings provide transparency for investors around executive-linked holdings in GSK’s U.S.-traded securities and reinforce governance practices expected of a major global biopharma issuer.

The most recent analyst rating on (GSK) stock is a Hold
with a $70.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GlaxoSmithKline stock,
see the GSK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GSK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GSK is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by solid financial performance (strong margins and cash generation, though with leverage and variability) and supportive valuation (low P/E with a ~3.5% dividend). This is meaningfully offset by weak technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD), while the latest earnings call was broadly constructive on guidance and specialty-led momentum but highlighted H2 phasing and launch/access headwinds.

To see Spark’s full report on GSK stock,
click here.

More about GlaxoSmithKline

GSK plc is a global biopharma company focused on uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease. The group develops and markets prescription medicines and vaccines across major therapeutic areas, with a strong presence in the U.S. market and listings that include American Depositary Shares on the New York Stock Exchange.

Headquartered in London and registered in England and Wales, GSK operates as one of the leading international pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical issuers. Its securities reporting obligations include regular filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission as a foreign private issuer under Form 20-F rules.

Average Trading Volume: 3,981,904

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $101.9B

data.title: Provident Financial Names CEO to Board, New Lending Chief
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An update from Provident Financial Holdings ( (PROV) ) is now available.

Provident Financial Holdings, Inc., the parent of Provident Savings Bank, F.S.B., announced that President and Chief Executive Officer Donavon P. Ternes will join the Boards of Directors of both the corporation and the bank beginning July 23, 2026, filling vacancies created by the death of Director William E. Thomas in April 2026. Ternes, who has served as CEO since January 2024 and has held progressively senior roles since joining in 2000, will serve on the boards until the corporation’s 2027 and the bank’s 2026 annual stockholder meetings.

The bank also appointed Michael S. Van Stockum, age 63, as Senior Vice President and Chief Lending Officer effective July 23, 2026, reflecting a consolidation of experienced leadership in its lending operations. Van Stockum, who has more than 40 years of lending and credit management experience and has been with the bank since 2007 in roles including Commercial Real Estate Loan Administrator and Community Reinvestment Act Officer, has no reportable related-party transactions or family relationships with current directors or executives, underscoring governance and compliance considerations for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (PROV) stock is a Hold
with a $17.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Provident Financial Holdings stock,
see the PROV Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PROV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PROV is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by moderate financial performance (profitability and cash conversion remain solid, but revenue and margins are weakening and ROE is modest). Technicals are supportive with a clear uptrend and positive momentum. Valuation is reasonable with a supportive dividend yield, while corporate events are mixed due to weaker reported earnings offset by buyback/dividend actions.

To see Spark’s full report on PROV stock,
click here.

More about Provident Financial Holdings

Provident Financial Holdings, Inc. is the holding company for Provident Savings Bank, F.S.B., a financial institution operating in the banking sector. The company focuses on providing a range of lending and deposit services through its bank subsidiary, serving customers across commercial, multifamily, construction and SBA lending markets, as well as traditional retail banking products.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $107.6M

data.title: TotalEnergies publishes comprehensive half-year 2026 financial report
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest update is out from TotalEnergies SE ( (FR:TTE) ).

TotalEnergies has released its half-year 2026 financial report, detailing consolidated results, segment performance and key operating metrics across exploration and production, LNG, power and downstream businesses. The report also presents data on environment-related indicators, including greenhouse gas emissions, production trends and realized prices for liquids and gas, as well as refining margins.

The document sets out adjusted net income, cash flow, profitability ratios and non-GAAP measures such as EBITDA, gearing and return on capital employed, offering a comprehensive view of the group’s financial health. It further outlines principal risks and uncertainties, related-party transactions and an outlook for the remainder of the year, providing investors and other stakeholders with an integrated assessment of TotalEnergies’ operational and financial positioning at mid-year 2026.

More about TotalEnergies SE

TotalEnergies SE is a global integrated energy company headquartered in Paris, operating across exploration and production, liquefied natural gas, power generation, refining and chemicals, and marketing and services. The group is actively repositioning its portfolio toward lower-carbon energy, while maintaining extensive upstream and downstream operations that make it a key player in international oil, gas and power markets.

Its diversified business segments include Exploration & Production, Integrated LNG, Integrated Power and Downstream activities, giving the company broad exposure to commodity cycles and refining margins. This integrated model underpins its financial performance, risk profile and strategic push into cleaner energy solutions alongside traditional hydrocarbons.

The latest update is out from Tiger ( (GB:AGI) ).

Potentially AI PLC, a London-headquartered collective AI company, is building an aggregator platform that gives users access to a wide range of powerful open-source and frontier AI models while preserving control over their data. The firm aims to support the emerging AI economy through upcoming launches of a consumer app, a professional workplace solution and a marketplace for users to develop, customise and monetise AI-driven products.

The company reported that all resolutions were approved at its latest Annual General Meeting, confirming shareholder support for its current strategy and planned product rollouts. The smooth passage of AGM resolutions underscores backing for Potentially’s growth plans in collective AI and its effort to broaden access to AI tools and economic opportunities for a wide base of users.

data.title: Tompkins Financial boosts dividend after record quarterly earnings
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from Tompkins Financial Corporation ( (TMP) ).

Tompkins Financial Corporation reported record earnings for the third consecutive quarter, with diluted earnings per share reaching $2.04 in the second quarter of 2026, a 36% increase from a year earlier and 12.1% higher than the first quarter. Net income for the quarter rose to $29.3 million, while first-half 2026 net income climbed 34.6% year over year to $55.4 million, driven by higher net interest income, stronger net interest margin and solid loan growth.

Net interest income grew 23% year over year in the second quarter to $74 million as net interest margin widened to 3.58%, supported by higher yields on earning assets and controlled funding costs, while average loans and deposits both expanded versus 2025. Despite a sharp drop in noninterest income following the sale of its insurance subsidiary in late 2025, Tompkins reduced noninterest expenses and maintained stable asset quality metrics, and its board on July 23, 2026 approved a 13% dividend increase, signaling confidence in the company’s capital strength and earnings momentum.

The most recent analyst rating on (TMP) stock is a Hold
with a $87.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tompkins Financial Corporation stock,
see the TMP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TMP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TMP is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong profitability and improved balance-sheet leverage, supported by a clear uptrend in the stock and favorable valuation (low P/E with a ~3% dividend). The main constraint is mixed cash conversion and flat TTM revenue growth, which adds uncertainty around sustaining the recent earnings strength.

To see Spark’s full report on TMP stock,
click here.

More about Tompkins Financial Corporation

Tompkins Financial Corporation, headquartered in Ithaca, N.Y., is a regional financial services company listed on the NYSE American under the ticker TMP. It focuses on community banking through lending and deposit services, with a loan portfolio concentrated in commercial real estate and commercial and industrial credits, and maintains a substantial base of low-cost core deposits to support its balance sheet and earnings profile.

data.title: ArcelorMittal posts Q2 2026 analyst consensus, distances itself from estimates
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

ArcelorMittal ( (NL:MT) ) has shared an update.

ArcelorMittal has published second-quarter 2026 sell-side analyst consensus figures compiled by independent data provider Visible Alpha, drawing on estimates from 13 of roughly 15 brokers that cover the stock. The aggregated consensus points to expected EBITDA of $2.04 billion, net income of $802 million and earnings per share of $1.06 for the quarter.

The company stresses that these forecasts reflect external analysts’ views rather than internal guidance, noting it neither collects nor validates the underlying data. By distancing itself from the consensus methodology and accuracy, ArcelorMittal signals that investors should treat the figures as an outside snapshot of market expectations rather than an official outlook on its forthcoming results.

The most recent analyst rating on (NL:MT) stock is a Hold
with a EUR57.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ArcelorMittal stock,
see the NL:MT Stock Forecast page.

More about ArcelorMittal

ArcelorMittal is one of the world’s leading integrated steel and mining companies, with operations in 60 countries and primary steelmaking in 14. It is the largest steel producer in Europe, a major player in the Americas, and is expanding in Asia via its AM/NS India joint venture, supplying automotive, engineering, construction and machinery customers while pursuing lower-carbon, higher-performance steels.

An announcement from Liberty Global A ( (LBTYA) ) is now available.

Liberty Global reported its second-quarter 2026 results on July 24, highlighting solid operational performance across its European telecom businesses, including broadband subscriber gains at VodafoneZiggo and Telenet and improved trading at Virgin Media O2, alongside continued fiber network expansion to 9 million premises in the UK and sustained mobile and broadband growth in Ireland. The company also advanced preparations for a planned Ziggo Group spin-off in 2027, exited its remaining EdgeConneX stake for $604 million, lifted year-to-date asset monetizations to about $1.2 billion and raised its year-end corporate cash target to roughly $2.0 billion, underscoring a strategy of asset rotation, balance-sheet strengthening and shareholder value focus.

Technicals further pressure the score due to a clear downtrend across moving averages and negative MACD. Offsetting these risks, the latest earnings call was moderately positive with reaffirmed 2026 guidance, improving broadband trends, and progress on strategic value-unlock initiatives, while valuation remains hard to assess given negative earnings and no dividend yield data.

data.title: HCA Healthcare Posts Solid Q2 2026 Results, Trims Outlook
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

HCA Healthcare ( (HCA) ) has provided an update.

HCA Healthcare reported its second-quarter 2026 results on July 24, 2026, posting an 8.7% year-on-year rise in revenue to $20.23 billion and a 2.8% increase in net income to $1.70 billion, with diluted EPS up 11.6% to $7.62 and adjusted EBITDA up 4.6% to $4.03 billion. Same-facility admissions rose 2.5% and ER visits 3.6%, while inpatient and outpatient surgeries declined, and the company also disclosed that first-half 2026 revenue reached $39.34 billion with net income of $3.32 billion.

The quarter was shaped by a negative payer mix shift from higher uninsured volumes tied to health insurance exchange coverage losses, which HCA estimated reduced pre-tax income by about $400 million, partially offset by roughly $400 million of incremental net benefit from Medicaid Supplemental Payment Programs, largely from Florida. Cash flow from operations fell sharply to $2.34 billion from $4.21 billion a year earlier, as HCA spent $1.23 billion on capital expenditures, repurchased 4.75 million shares for $2.06 billion, and maintained sizeable debt of $49.72 billion, while its board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.78 per share and the company trimmed full-year 2026 guidance to reflect greater exchange-related headwinds and higher expected Medicaid support.

The most recent analyst rating on (HCA) stock is a Hold
with a $369.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on HCA Healthcare stock,
see the HCA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HCA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HCA is a Neutral.

The score is anchored by strong earnings and cash generation but is pulled down by elevated balance-sheet risk (negative equity) and weak technical trend (price below all key moving averages with negative momentum). Valuation is a supportive factor (P/E 12.43), while recent updates and the earnings call show resilience but highlight meaningful payer-mix/exchange headwinds and slightly lower 2026 guidance.

To see Spark’s full report on HCA stock,
click here.

More about HCA Healthcare

HCA Healthcare, Inc. is a large U.S. hospital operator, running acute care hospitals and related healthcare facilities with a focus on inpatient, outpatient and emergency services. The company generates revenue primarily from patient admissions and procedures, and its performance is closely tied to payer mix dynamics, government programs and broader health policy developments affecting reimbursement.

Average Trading Volume: 1,621,059

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $82.5B

For an in-depth examination of HCA stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.
